name: Pepper
intro: 1/2 teaspoon ground alligator pepper or black pepper 1 tablespoons ground cayenne or cameroon pepper 1 teaspoon ground uda (grains of selim)

ingredients.item: thick rounds of yam] (peeled and cut into bite-sized pieces)
1/2 teaspoon ground alligator pepper or black pepper
ground/grated ehuru seed (calabash nutmeg) 1/2 teaspoon ground uziza seeds
mushrooms or roasted tofu (cut into bite-size pieces)

method: In a large pot, place the diced yam, onions, ground pepper, cayenne/ cameroon pepper, uda, ehuru, uziza seeds, ogiri and palm oil. Cover with water and season with salt to taste. Bring to a boil then cover and simmer on medium-high heat for 15 minutes until the yam is fork-tender.
Add the utazi leaves and mushrooms or tofu. Cook for another 5 minutes. Stir well and serve hot.
